questions.prompt: A field already contains "old". You call sendKeys("new") without clearing. What is in the field?
options: An error is thrown because the field is not empty
The field is cleared automatically before typing
questions.explanation: sendKeys appends to the existing value, producing "oldnew". Call clear() first when you need the field to hold only the new text.

questions.prompt: What does getText() return for an element?
options: The element's raw HTML including tags
The value of its id attribute
Its visible, trimmed text as the user sees it
questions.explanation: getText() returns the rendered, visible text of the element and its children, with surrounding whitespace trimmed.

questions.prompt: A Submit button is on screen but greyed out until a checkbox is ticked. Which is true before ticking?
options: isDisplayed() is false and isEnabled() is false
isDisplayed() is true and isEnabled() is false
isDisplayed() is false and isEnabled() is true
Both are always true regardless of the disabled state
questions.explanation: The button is visible, so isDisplayed() is true, but it is disabled, so isEnabled() is false. The two methods answer different questions.

questions.prompt: Which method types characters into an input field the way a user would?
questions.explanation: sendKeys() simulates keyboard input into the element. setText, type, and fill are not the Selenium WebElement methods for this.
